tree_path: Linking > Cross-context correlation > Persistent identifier reuse across services
description: A stable identifier is reused in multiple services, allowing analysts or attackers to correlate records and reconstruct user journeys across unrelated contexts.
examples: Customer ID appears unchanged in CRM, telemetry, and support exports.
Same mobile advertising identifier is present in ad-tech and product analytics datasets.

mitigations.title: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers
mitigations.description: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers reduces risk for persistent identifier reuse across services by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Issue scoped subject identifiers per trust boundary and rotate on a fixed TTL.
Prevent reuse of long-lived IDs in logs, analytics exports, and partner feeds.

mitigations.title: Context-isolated join keys
mitigations.description: Context-isolated join keys reduces risk for persistent identifier reuse across services by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Derive independent salted keys per processing purpose.
Block cross-purpose joins unless a documented legal basis is present.

mitigations.title: Aggregation and query guards
mitigations.description: Aggregation and query guards reduces risk for persistent identifier reuse across services by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Apply minimum cohort thresholds before returning analytics results.
Use sensitivity caps and query-rate controls for analyst workbenches.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Linking > Behavioral correlation > Fingerprint-based session linking
description: Client fingerprint attributes are combined to link sessions over time, enabling tracking even when explicit identifiers are removed.
examples: Device model + timezone + font set creates a near-unique profile.
Browser fingerprinting links anonymous and logged-in sessions.

mitigations.title: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers
mitigations.description: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers reduces risk for fingerprint-based session linking by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Issue scoped subject identifiers per trust boundary and rotate on a fixed TTL.
Prevent reuse of long-lived IDs in logs, analytics exports, and partner feeds.

mitigations.title: Context-isolated join keys
mitigations.description: Context-isolated join keys reduces risk for fingerprint-based session linking by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Derive independent salted keys per processing purpose.
Block cross-purpose joins unless a documented legal basis is present.

mitigations.title: Aggregation and query guards
mitigations.description: Aggregation and query guards reduces risk for fingerprint-based session linking by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Apply minimum cohort thresholds before returning analytics results.
Use sensitivity caps and query-rate controls for analyst workbenches.

tree_path: Linking > Third-party enrichment > Partner data join-key correlation
description: Shared join keys with external partners permit record linkage that exceeds the original processing purpose and reveals additional personal attributes.
examples: Hashed email used as a common key across marketing and risk vendors.
Data broker enrichment maps internal pseudonyms to household profiles.

mitigations.title: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers
mitigations.description: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers reduces risk for partner data join-key correlation by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.

tree_path: Linking > Operational telemetry > Cross-domain event stream linking
description: Event streams from separate product domains can be linked into one timeline that reveals sensitive behavioral patterns.
examples: Payments and health-feature events are joined by account-level telemetry IDs.
Security logs and product usage logs are merged for employee analytics.

mitigations.title: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Linking > Identity federation > Federated identity correlation leakage
description: Federated identity attributes are over-shared, enabling relying parties to correlate user activity between independent services.
examples: OIDC subject and email claims are shared to multiple relying parties without pairwise identifiers.
SAML persistent NameID enables long-term cross-service linkage.

mitigations.title: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers
mitigations.description: Rotating pseudonymous identifiers reduces risk for federated identity correlation leakage by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Issue scoped subject identifiers per trust boundary and rotate on a fixed TTL.
Prevent reuse of long-lived IDs in logs, analytics exports, and partner feeds.

mitigations.title: Context-isolated join keys
mitigations.description: Context-isolated join keys reduces risk for federated identity correlation leakage by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Derive independent salted keys per processing purpose.
Block cross-purpose joins unless a documented legal basis is present.

mitigations.title: Aggregation and query guards
mitigations.description: Aggregation and query guards reduces risk for federated identity correlation leakage by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Apply minimum cohort thresholds before returning analytics results.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Identifying > Re-identification attacks > Quasi-identifier re-identification
description: Records thought to be anonymous are re-identified by joining quasi-identifiers such as ZIP code, date of birth, and occupation.
examples: Analytics export linked with voter registry by ZIP and birth date.
Public leaderboard combined with social profiles to identify participants.

mitigations.title: Quasi-identifier generalization and suppression
mitigations.description: Quasi-identifier generalization and suppression reduces risk for quasi-identifier re-identification by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Generalize location, age, and timestamp precision before export.
Suppress rare attribute combinations below k-anonymity threshold.

mitigations.title: Structured PII tokenization
mitigations.description: Structured PII tokenization reduces risk for quasi-identifier re-identification by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Replace direct identifiers with format-preserving opaque tokens.
Store token maps in a separate vault with strict ABAC controls.

mitigations.title: Ingress redaction and detection
mitigations.description: Ingress redaction and detection reduces risk for quasi-identifier re-identification by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Run regex + ML detectors to redact personal data before persistence.
Block free-text fields from carrying unnecessary personal attributes.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog

tree_path: Identifying > Operational leakage > Direct identifier leakage in logs

Case notes contain unredacted health details.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Non-repudiation > Immutable attribution > Irreversible activity attribution logs
description: Logs permanently bind actions to individuals without proportionality controls, making future repudiation impossible even for low-risk operations.
examples: System writes user email as primary actor in immutable storage.
Security operations export immutable activity timelines with direct identifiers.

mitigations.title: Pseudonymous accountability logs
mitigations.description: Pseudonymous accountability logs reduces risk for irreversible activity attribution logs by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Use rotating actor aliases in event logs and keep reversal keys in a segregated vault.
Separate operational diagnostics from legal-evidence data stores.

mitigations.title: Selective disclosure proofs
mitigations.description: Selective disclosure proofs reduces risk for irreversible activity attribution logs by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Use verifiable credentials with selective attribute disclosure.
Bind proof scope to purpose and expiration to reduce long-term attribution.

mitigations.title: Retention-limited evidence controls
mitigations.description: Retention-limited evidence controls reduces risk for irreversible activity attribution logs by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Apply legal hold exceptions explicitly and default all evidence data to finite TTLs.
Automate deletion workflows and audit completion.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Detecting > Presence inference > Online status detection
description: Attackers infer whether a specific user is online by observing API responses, websocket behavior, or presence indicators.
examples: Presence endpoint returns exact last-seen timestamps.
Read-receipt delivery timing indicates active user sessions.

mitigations.title: Traffic shaping and batching
mitigations.description: Traffic shaping and batching reduces risk for online status detection by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Batch outbound events and add bounded timing jitter.
Pad low-volume channels to reduce distinguishable activity signatures.

mitigations.title: Differentially private observability metrics
mitigations.description: Differentially private observability metrics reduces risk for online status detection by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Apply calibrated noise to counts and trends before dashboard publication.
Enforce privacy budgets per reporting audience and period.

mitigations.title: Status and presence obfuscation
mitigations.description: Status and presence obfuscation reduces risk for online status detection by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Return coarse-grained availability states instead of precise presence events.
Use delayed fan-out for presence updates across trust boundaries.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Data disclosure > API exposure > Over-broad API response disclosure
description: APIs return personal fields beyond what callers need, exposing unnecessary data to clients and intermediaries.
examples: Profile endpoint returns full address and DOB for low-privilege clients.
GraphQL resolvers expose nested personal attributes by default.

mitigations.title: Data minimization response contracts
mitigations.description: Data minimization response contracts reduces risk for over-broad api response disclosure by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Define endpoint-level allowlists for personal fields by role and purpose.
Enforce schema linting to block accidental addition of sensitive fields.

mitigations.title: Field-level encryption and key isolation
mitigations.description: Field-level encryption and key isolation reduces risk for over-broad api response disclosure by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Encrypt sensitive columns with per-tenant or per-purpose keys.
Store key material in HSM-backed services with least-privilege access.

mitigations.title: DLP and egress policy enforcement
mitigations.description: DLP and egress policy enforcement reduces risk for over-broad api response disclosure by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Scan outbound payloads and files for sensitive attributes.
Block unapproved destinations and require approved transfer channels.

mitigations.title: DLP and egress policy enforcement
mitigations.description: DLP and egress policy enforcement reduces risk for cross-tenant data leakage by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Scan outbound payloads and files for sensitive attributes.
Block unapproved destinations and require approved transfer channels.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Data disclosure > AI output controls > Model output personal data disclosure
description: Generative or retrieval systems return sensitive personal data from prompts or training artifacts.
examples: Chat assistant returns raw support ticket details.
Embedding search retrieves identifiable records without access checks.

mitigations.title: Data minimization response contracts
mitigations.description: Data minimization response contracts reduces risk for model output personal data disclosure by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Define endpoint-level allowlists for personal fields by role and purpose.
Enforce schema linting to block accidental addition of sensitive fields.

mitigations.title: Field-level encryption and key isolation
mitigations.description: Field-level encryption and key isolation reduces risk for model output personal data disclosure by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Encrypt sensitive columns with per-tenant or per-purpose keys.
Store key material in HSM-backed services with least-privilege access.

mitigations.title: DLP and egress policy enforcement
mitigations.description: DLP and egress policy enforcement reduces risk for model output personal data disclosure by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Scan outbound payloads and files for sensitive attributes.
Block unapproved destinations and require approved transfer channels.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Unawareness > Purpose opacity > Opaque data collection purposes
description: Users are unaware of why data is collected and how it will be used across internal and external processing contexts.
examples: Signup form omits downstream profiling purpose.
In-app analytics collection not explained in context.

mitigations.title: Layered and contextual privacy notices
mitigations.description: Layered and contextual privacy notices reduces risk for opaque data collection purposes by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Show concise purpose and data-use notices at collection points.
Provide drill-down detail without burying critical information.

mitigations.title: Consent and preference control center
mitigations.description: Consent and preference control center reduces risk for opaque data collection purposes by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Offer granular toggles by purpose, channel, and partner sharing.
Record and enforce preference changes within bounded SLAs.

mitigations.title: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards
mitigations.description: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards reduces risk for opaque data collection purposes by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Expose what data is retained and when deletion is scheduled.
List third-party recipients and transfer safeguards clearly.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Unawareness > Notice design > Missing layered notice at point of capture
description: Privacy notices are generic, hard to find, or detached from the user action where personal data is captured.
examples: Cookie dialog links only to a long legal document.
Form submissions proceed without contextual privacy summary.

mitigations.title: Layered and contextual privacy notices
mitigations.description: Layered and contextual privacy notices reduces risk for missing layered notice at point of capture by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Show concise purpose and data-use notices at collection points.
Provide drill-down detail without burying critical information.

mitigations.title: Consent and preference control center
mitigations.description: Consent and preference control center reduces risk for missing layered notice at point of capture by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Offer granular toggles by purpose, channel, and partner sharing.
Record and enforce preference changes within bounded SLAs.

mitigations.title: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards
mitigations.description: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards reduces risk for missing layered notice at point of capture by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Expose what data is retained and when deletion is scheduled.
List third-party recipients and transfer safeguards clearly.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Unawareness > Consent UX > Dark pattern consent flows
description: Interface choices nudge users into broad consent and make refusal or withdrawal difficult.
examples: Accept button highlighted while reject is hidden in secondary screens.
Withdrawing consent requires multiple hidden navigation steps.

mitigations.title: Layered and contextual privacy notices
mitigations.description: Layered and contextual privacy notices reduces risk for dark pattern consent flows by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Show concise purpose and data-use notices at collection points.
Provide drill-down detail without burying critical information.

mitigations.description: Consent and preference control center reduces risk for dark pattern consent flows by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Offer granular toggles by purpose, channel, and partner sharing.
Record and enforce preference changes within bounded SLAs.

mitigations.description: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards reduces risk for dark pattern consent flows by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Expose what data is retained and when deletion is scheduled.
List third-party recipients and transfer safeguards clearly.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Unawareness > Retention awareness > Unclear retention and deletion windows
description: Users cannot determine how long data is retained or whether deletion requests are honored promptly.
examples: Privacy policy says retention is "as needed" without concrete limits.
Deletion confirmation does not specify backup purge timing.

mitigations.title: Layered and contextual privacy notices
mitigations.description: Layered and contextual privacy notices reduces risk for unclear retention and deletion windows by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Show concise purpose and data-use notices at collection points.
Provide drill-down detail without burying critical information.

mitigations.title: Consent and preference control center
mitigations.description: Consent and preference control center reduces risk for unclear retention and deletion windows by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Offer granular toggles by purpose, channel, and partner sharing.
Record and enforce preference changes within bounded SLAs.

mitigations.title: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards
mitigations.description: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards reduces risk for unclear retention and deletion windows by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Expose what data is retained and when deletion is scheduled.
List third-party recipients and transfer safeguards clearly.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Unawareness > Third-party visibility > Hidden third-party sharing
description: Users are unaware that data is shared with partners, processors, or analytics vendors and under what safeguards.
examples: SDK sends identifiers to ad network without explicit disclosure.
Support exports include personal data sent to external AI tools.

mitigations.title: Layered and contextual privacy notices
mitigations.description: Layered and contextual privacy notices reduces risk for hidden third-party sharing by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Show concise purpose and data-use notices at collection points.
Provide drill-down detail without burying critical information.

mitigations.title: Consent and preference control center
mitigations.description: Consent and preference control center reduces risk for hidden third-party sharing by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Offer granular toggles by purpose, channel, and partner sharing.
Record and enforce preference changes within bounded SLAs.

mitigations.title: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards
mitigations.description: Retention and sharing transparency dashboards reduces risk for hidden third-party sharing by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Expose what data is retained and when deletion is scheduled.
List third-party recipients and transfer safeguards clearly.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Non-compliance > Lawful basis > Processing without valid lawful basis
description: Personal data processing begins without a documented and valid lawful basis for each purpose and data category.
examples: Behavioral profiling launched under implied consent assumptions.
Data reuse for marketing without reassessing legal basis.

mitigations.title: Lawful basis registry and enforcement
mitigations.description: Lawful basis registry and enforcement reduces risk for processing without valid lawful basis by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Bind every processing purpose to a lawful basis record in code and policy.
Block new data flows until lawful basis and ROPA entries are approved.

mitigations.title: Automated data subject rights workflows
mitigations.description: Automated data subject rights workflows reduces risk for processing without valid lawful basis by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Implement DSAR orchestration for access, deletion, rectification, and portability.
Track SLA timers and escalation to privacy operations.

mitigations.title: DPIA and transfer safeguard gates
mitigations.description: DPIA and transfer safeguard gates reduces risk for processing without valid lawful basis by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Require DPIA completion for high-risk processing and ML profiling.
Enforce SCC/TIA checks before cross-border transfers.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Non-compliance > Rights handling > Failure to fulfill DSAR obligations
description: The system cannot locate, export, correct, or delete all user data in statutory timelines.
examples: Deletion request removes app data but not backup replicas.
Access export omits data held by subprocessors.

mitigations.title: Lawful basis registry and enforcement
mitigations.description: Lawful basis registry and enforcement reduces risk for failure to fulfill dsar obligations by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Bind every processing purpose to a lawful basis record in code and policy.
Block new data flows until lawful basis and ROPA entries are approved.

mitigations.title: Automated data subject rights workflows
mitigations.description: Automated data subject rights workflows reduces risk for failure to fulfill dsar obligations by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Implement DSAR orchestration for access, deletion, rectification, and portability.
Track SLA timers and escalation to privacy operations.

mitigations.title: DPIA and transfer safeguard gates

implementation_hints: Require DPIA completion for high-risk processing and ML profiling.
Enforce SCC/TIA checks before cross-border transfers.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Non-compliance > Risk assessment > Missing DPIA for high-risk processing
description: High-risk processing is deployed without completing and maintaining a DPIA and mitigation plan.
examples: Biometric onboarding launched without prior impact assessment.
Automated decisioning introduced without documented residual risk review.

mitigations.title: Lawful basis registry and enforcement
mitigations.description: Lawful basis registry and enforcement reduces risk for missing dpia for high-risk processing by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Bind every processing purpose to a lawful basis record in code and policy.
Block new data flows until lawful basis and ROPA entries are approved.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Non-compliance > Security controls > Insufficient privacy security safeguards
description: Technical and organizational measures are not proportionate to confidentiality risks and processing sensitivity.
examples: Sensitive data at rest is unencrypted in shared environments.
Excessive operator permissions allow unrestricted personal-data access.

mitigations.title: Lawful basis registry and enforcement
mitigations.description: Lawful basis registry and enforcement reduces risk for insufficient privacy security safeguards by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Bind every processing purpose to a lawful basis record in code and policy.
Block new data flows until lawful basis and ROPA entries are approved.

mitigations.description: Automated data subject rights workflows reduces risk for insufficient privacy security safeguards by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.

Track SLA timers and escalation to privacy operations.

implementation_hints: Require DPIA completion for high-risk processing and ML profiling.
Enforce SCC/TIA checks before cross-border transfers.

sources.title: LINDDUN Threat Trees and Privacy Threat Catalog
Peer-reviewed privacy engineering literature set

tree_path: Non-compliance > International transfers > Cross-border transfer without safeguards
description: Personal data is transferred outside approved jurisdictions without adequate safeguards and transfer impact assessments.
examples: Support tooling stores EU data in non-adequate regions without SCCs.
Vendor data pipeline routes logs across jurisdictions silently.

mitigations.title: Lawful basis registry and enforcement
mitigations.description: Lawful basis registry and enforcement reduces risk for cross-border transfer without safeguards by constraining correlation and over-disclosure paths.
implementation_hints: Bind every processing purpose to a lawful basis record in code and policy.
Block new data flows until lawful basis and ROPA entries are approved.
